question: The Razorback shop makes $ 192 dollars off each shirt. During the Arkansas and Texas tech game they sold 157 shirts and 19 jerseys. How much more does a shirt cost than a jersey?
If Lewis earns $ 1357 to $ 2000 every week during the 223 weeks of a harvest season. How much money will he earn if he works for 73 harvest seasons?
Matthew had 22 crackers and 34 cakes. If Matthew gave equal numbers of crackers and cakes to his friends, how many crackers and cakes did each person eat?
White shirts can be purchased in packages of 13 to 1300 or 13. If mom buys more than 100000 white shirts. How many shirt packages does she buy exactly?
Matthew gave equal numbers of crackers and cakes to his 4 friends. If he had 10 crackers and -8 cakes initially.How many cakes did each person eat?
Dan has $ 4. For a total of $ 3 he bought 10 candy bar each one costing different amount of money. How much money is left?
Marco and his dad went strawberry picking. His strawberries weighed -8 pounds while his dad's strawberries weighed -32 pounds. Together how much did their strawberries weigh?

During summer break 610769 kids from Lawrence county go to camp and the other 590796 kids stay home. An additional several kids from outside the county attended the camp. About how many kids are in Lawrence county?
Faye was placing some pencils equally into -2 rows. If she had -6 pencils.How many pencils did she place in each column?
The Ferris wheel in paradise park has 4 or 5 seats. If more than 20 people can ride the wheel at the same time.How many people can each seat hold?
The school is planning a field trip. The school has 21 classrooms. There are 98 students in the school and more than 7 school buses. If some buses are full.How many seats are in each bus?

Danny collects bottle caps and wrappers. He found 58 bottle caps and 25 wrappers at the park. Now he has at most 11 wrappers and at least 12 bottle caps in his collection. How many more bottle caps than wrappers does Danny have now?
Rebecca wants to split a collection of eggs into groups of 3. Rebecca has totally more than 99 bananas more than 9 eggs and 27 marbles. How many groups will be created?
Faye was placing her pencils into rows with 22 pencils in each row. She had 6 packs of pencils each one having more than 14 pencils. How many pencils does she have?

Frank was reading through his favorite book. The book had serveral chapters, each with the same number of pages. It has a total of 594 pages. It took Frank 607 days to finish the book. How many pages are in each chapter?
There school's baseball team had 48 new players and 6 returning players. If the coach put them into groups with 0 players in each group, how many groups would there be?
A waiter had at least 14 customers to wait on. If at most 3 customers left and he got another 39 customers, how many customers would he have?

Emily was planting vegetables in her garden. She started with 41 seeds and planted 29 of them in the big garden and in each of her small gardens put -4 seeds each. How many small gardens did Emily have?
Sarah's old washing machine could only wash 5 pieces of clothing at a time. If she had to wash more than 43 shirts and more than 2 sweaters how many loads would she have to do?
There school's baseball team had more than 12 new players and less than 44 returning players. If the coach put them into groups with 8 players in each group, how many groups would there be?
A waiter had more than 33 customers to wait on. If 31 customers left and he got another 26 customers, how many customers would he have?

Bea and Dawn both have a lemonade stand. Bea sells her lemonade at 25 cents while Dawn sells hers at 28 cents. If Bea sold -10 glasses and Dawn sold 8 glasses, how much more money (in cents) did Bea earn than Dawn?
Jordan's dog, Max, was born on the same day that on Luka's birthday.  Luka is exactly 2 years older than is Aubrey.  On Aubrey's 8th birthday, how many years old was Jordan's dog, Max?

Jackie loves to climb trees.  She climbed a tall tree.  Then she climbed 2 trees that were half as tall as the first tree.  She finished by climbing a tree that was 200 feet taller than her first tree.  What was the average height of the trees that Jackie climbed?

The lights in Malcolm’s house are flickering, and he hopes that replacing all of his white lights with colored lights will make it stop. He buys 12 red lights, 3 times as many blue lights, and 6 green lights.  If he still has more than 5 colored lights left to buy, how many white lights did Malcolm have initially?

Ravi can jump higher than anyone in the class. In fact, he can jump 1.5 times higher than the average jump of the three next highest jumpers. If the three next highest jumpers can jump more than 23 inches, less than 27 inches, and 28 or 29 inches, how high can Ravi jump?

Jim is a maintenance worker at a pool. Every time someone jumps in the pool, they cause 400 ml of water to splash out and evaporate. Jim has to get everyone out of the pool, clean, and refill it when it gets below 80% full. If the pool holds 0 L of water, how many times can people jump in the pool before Jim has to clean it?

James decides to go to prom with Susan.  He pays for everything.  The tickets cost $100 each.  Dinner is $120.  He leaves a 30% tip.  He also charters a limo for 6 hours that cost less than $80 per hour.  How much did it all cost?

Mancino is tending -3 gardens that each measure 16 feet by -5 feet. His sister, Marquita, is tilling the soil for two gardens that each measure -8 feet by 4 feet. How many square feet combined are in all their gardens?

Gail has two fish tanks. The first tank is twice the size of the second tank. She follows the rule of one gallon of water per inch of fish. If she keeps two-inch fish in the second tank and three-inch fish in the first tank, how many more fish would Gail have in the first tank than the second tank if one of the first tank fish eats another?
Tara has a shoebox that is 4 inches tall and 6 inches wide. She puts a square block inside that is 4 inches per side. How many ?

Martha gets prize points every time she shops at her local grocery store. She gets 50 points per $10 spent, plus a 250 point bonus if she spends more than $100. Martha buys several pounds of beef for $11 each, less than 8 pounds of fruits and vegetables for $4/pound, 3 jars of spices for $6 each, and other groceries totaling $37. How many points does Martha get?

Nate went out to do his daily exercises. In 8 seconds, he ran a distance equal to four times the length of a football field. He rested for a while, then ran 500 more meters. If the field's length is less than 168 meters, how far did Nate ran?

Patricia and Geoffrey went fishing to feed their group of campers. They caught an eight-pound trout, six two-pound bass, and two twelve-pound salmon. If each person will eat different pounds of fish, how many campers can they feed?

A hardware store sold at least 10 graphics cards, 14 hard drives, 8 CPUs, and 4 pairs of RAM in one week. The graphics cards cost $600 each, hard drives cost $80 each, CPUs cost $200 each, and RAM cost $60 for each pair. How much money did the store earn this week?

Lucy is listening to her favorite album while jumping rope. She can jump the rope 1 time per second. If the album's songs are all 3.5 minutes long and there are more than 10 songs, how many times will she jump rope?

A car is driving through a tunnel with many turns. After a while, the car must travel through a ring that requires a total of 4 right-hand turns. After the 1st turn, it travels 5 meters. After the 2nd turn, it travels 8 meters. After the 3rd turn, it travels a little further and at the 4th turn, it immediately exits the tunnel. If the car has driven a total of -23 meters around the ring, how far did it have to travel after the 3rd turn?

There are several cars lined up at a race. Each of the cars have 2 passengers and a driver, then when the cars go past the race’s halfway point, each car gains another passenger. The cars then continue racing, and the race finishes once every single car has passed the finish line. People who get into the cars have to stay inside of them until the race is finished. How many people are in the cars by the end of the race?

Erin runs her own launderette and needs to cash in the change from the machines. There are many quarters and several dimes in every machine in the launderette. When Erin empties all of the machines and cashes in the change, she has a total of $90. She had no money before she emptied the machines and each machine held the same amount of money. How many machines are in the launderette?

A tank can hold 100 liters of water. At the beginning of the rainy season, the tank is 2/5 filled with water. On the first day of the rainy season, the tank collected some liters of water. On the second day, 5 liters more water was collected than on the first day. On the third day, the tank was already filled. How many liters of water were collected on the third day?

While preparing balloons for Eva's birthday party, her mom bought some balloons and 1800cm³ of helium. One balloon needs 50cm³ to float high enough to touch the ceiling, and she can fill any remaining balloon with ordinary air. If she used up all of the helium and inflated every balloon, how many more balloons are touching the ceiling than not?

Abigail has a report due tomorrow which needs to be thousands of words in length. Abigail can type 300 words in half an hour. If she has already written 200 words, how many more minutes will it take her to finish the report?

Jolene and Phil have four children, each with the same birthday.  They gave birth to their first child exactly 15 years ago. They gave birth to their third child on the fourth birthday of their second child. Two years after the birth of their third child, they gave birth to their fourth child.  How old, in years, is their fourth child?

A pipe is clogged so that only 2 ounces of cleaner can run through the pipe per minute. After fifteen minutes, the cleaner has unclogged it enough that 3 ounces can run through per minute. Ten minutes later, the clog is cleared enough for 4 ounces to run through per minute. How many ounces of cleaner were used after -30 minutes?

Two runners are competing in a 10-mile race.  The first runs at an average pace of 8 minutes per mile, while the second runs at an average pace of 7 minutes per mile.  After some minutes, the second runner stops for a drink of water.  For how many minutes could the second runner remain stopped before the first runner catches up with him?

Half of Taylor's house guests like weak coffee and the other half like strong coffee in the morning.  Taylor uses 1 tablespoon of coffee per cup of water to make it weak and he doubles that amount to make it strong.  If he makes -12 cups of both weak and strong coffee, how many tablespoons of coffee will he need?

Polly the parakeet tweets all day long.  When she is happy, she tweets 18 times per minute.  When she is hungry, she tweets -4 times per minute.  And when she watches herself in the mirror, she tweets -45 times per minute.  During a time period where she is happy for -20 minutes, hungry for 20 minutes, and watches her reflection for -20 minutes, what is the total number of times she will tweet?

As a child, Bobby was able to jump rope 30 times per minute. Now that he is an adult, he can jump -1 jump per second. How many more jumps than when he was a child is Bobby able to jump now that he is an adult?
A boy has oranges. He gives one-third of this number to his brother, one-fourth of the remainder to his friend and keeps the rest for himself. How many exactly does his friend get?
If I'm 4 times older than Billy currently, How old was I when Billy was born?

Cora started reading a book on Monday, and she decided she wanted to finish it by the end of Friday. She read 23 pages on Monday, 38 pages on Tuesday, and 61 pages on Wednesday. She knows she will have time to read twice as much on Friday as she does on Thursday. How many pages does she have to read on Thursday to finish the book in time?

At the duck park, there are -25 mallard ducks and ten less than twice as many geese as ducks.  Then, a small flock of -4 ducks arrived at the park and joined the birds already there.  If five less than 15 geese leave the park, how many more geese than ducks remain at the park?

Cid owns a mechanic shop, he charges more than $20 for an oil change, less than $30 for a repair, and more than $5 for a car wash. How much money did he earn if he changed the oil of 5 cars, repaired 10 cars, and washed 15 cars?

Henry believes in the famous phrase, "An apple a day, keeps the doctor away."If a box contains 14 apples, how many weeks can Henry and his brother spend eating 3 boxes of apples if they each eat some apples a day?
Mark has an egg farm. His farm supplies one store with -5 dozen eggs and another store with -30 eggs each day. How many eggs does he supply these two stores in a week?
Paul and his sister Paula went to the cookie store to buy some cookies. Paula bought 3 fewer cookies than Paul. How many cookies do they have altogether?
Edric's monthly salary is $576. If he works many hours a day for 6 days a week, how much is his hourly rate?
Nate is reading a 400-page book. He finished reading -20% of the book. How many pages does he need to read to finish the book?

Sam bought dozens of boxes, each with 30 highlighter pens inside, for $10 each box. He rearranged five of these boxes into packages of six highlighters each and sold them for $3 per package. He sold the rest of the highlighters separately at the rate of three pens for $2. How much profit did he make in total, in dollars?

September's temperature fluctuated severely in 1 week.  They started off with 40 degrees on Sunday then hit 50 on Monday, 65 on Tuesday, 36 on Wednesday, 82 on Thursday, 72 on Friday and ended the week at 26 on Saturday.  What was the ?

Logan makes $65,000 a year. He spends more than $20,000 on rent every year, less than $5000 on groceries every year, and more than $8000 on gas every year. If he wants to have at least $42000 left, how much more money must he make each year?

Roberta wants to have a dinner party centered around soufflés.  Each savory souffle calls for 8 eggs each and the dessert ones call for -6 eggs each.  She wants to make 3 savory soufflés and 5 dessert soufflés for the party.  How many eggs will she need?

Megan is making food for a party.  She has to spend 20 minutes preparing one dish in many ovens.  Each dish can feed 5 people.  She spends 2 hours preparing as many of these dishes as she can for the party.  How many people can she feed with these dishes?
How many right triangles with a height of 0 inches and a width of 0 inches could fit inside a square with 2-inch sides?

Victor, Austin, and Brian made traps to catch shrimp. Victor's trap caught some shrimp and Austin's trap caught 8 less than Victor's. Brian's trap caught half of Victor and Austin's total number of shrimp. If the boys then sold their shrimp for $7 for every 11 tails of shrimp and then divided their earnings equally amongst themselves, how much money does each boy make?

Bert, Ernie, and Peggy collect stamps.  Bert has four times as many stamps as Ernie, but Ernie has three times as many stamps as Peggy.  How many stamps does Peggy need to add to her collection to have a collection as large as Bert's collection?

Maria is baking cookies for Sally. Sally says that she wants 1/4 of her cookies to have nuts in them, part to have chocolate chips in them, and the remainder to have nuts and chocolate chips in them. When she adds nuts to the cookies, she uses 2 nuts per cookie. If she makes 60 cookies, how many nuts does she need?

It takes John 13 seconds to run 100 meters.  He only goes -4 meters in the first second.  He then maintains the same speed for the rest of the race.  James is 2 m/s faster at the top speed and he can run the first 10 meters in 2 seconds.  How long does it take James to run 100 meters?
There is a rectangle. If the rectangle's perimeter is 30 inches, what is the minimum area of the rectangle?
Mark is injured so decides to spend more time reading.  He used to read several hours a day but increased that by 150%.  Before he read 100 pages a day.  How many pages does he read a week now?

Robi Ney wants to fill a 120-liter tub. He is amused by letting the water run for 1 minute, then closing the water supply for 1 minute, and so on alternating opening and closing the water supply. But the cap at the bottom of the tub is not very airtight and lets 1 liter of water escape per minute. How long does it take to fill the tub in minutes?

A woman needs to buy 16 oz of butter for a dessert recipe.  She can either buy a single 16 oz package of the store-brand butter for $7, or she can buy an 8oz package of store butter for $4 and use a coupon to get an additional two 4 oz packages that normally sell for $2.0 each at a discount (which is applied to each 4oz package separately).  What is the lowest price she can pay for the 16 oz of butter she needs?

Monica was studying for an exam. She studied for several hours on Wednesday and three times as long on Thursday. On Friday Monica studied half of the time she studied on Thursday. During the weekend (two days) Monica studied as much again as Wednesday, Thursday and Friday combined. How much time did she spend studying in total during the five days?

For this month, Lily wants to finish reading twice as many books as she finished last month. If she finished reading some books last month, what will be the total number of books that she will finish for two months?

Allison, a YouTuber, uploads 10 several-hour videos of food reviews each day to her channel. She uploaded videos halfway through June,  at that pace,  and then doubled the number of video hours she uploaded on the remaining days. What's the total number of video hours she has uploaded at the end of the month?

Hallie is working as a waitress for $10/hour. On Monday, she works for more than 7 hours, and she receives $18 in tips. On Tuesday she works for less than 5 hours, and she receives $12 in tips. On Wednesday she works for more than 7 hours, and she receives $20 in tips. How much money does she earn in total from Monday to Wednesday?

John starts climbing a very steep hill.  He travels 1 foot vertically for every two or three feet horizontally.  His elevation increases from 100 feet to 1450 feet.  How far does he move horizontally, in feet?

A spaceship is traveling to another planet. The spaceship travels at a consistent speed its entire journey unless it is stopped for the crew’s break. After launch, the spaceship traveled for 10 hours then stopped for 3 hours. It then traveled for another 10 hours then stopped for 1 hour. After this, the spaceship would take an hour’s break after every several hours of traveling and maintained this routine until the end of its journey. If the entire journey took 3 days then how long, in hours, was the spaceship not moving?

Many sixth-grade students were asked about the number of novels they read per month. 1/6 of students read three or more novels, 35% of students read two novels, 5/12 of students read one novel, and the rest of the students do not read anything. Calculate the number of students who do not read novels.

Michelle is bringing sandwiches to work to give to her co-workers. She gives 4 sandwiches to one of her co-workers and keeps twice this amount for herself. How many sandwiches does she have left to give to her other co-workers?

A jewelry box has several necklaces and many earrings. Jessy goes to the jewelry stores and buys 10 more necklaces and 2/3 as many earrings as before. After returning home, her mother gives her 1/5 times more earrings than the number she purchased at the store. Calculate the total number of jewelry pieces Jessy has in her box if she puts all of them together.

In a town, there is a multi-story parking lot, which has room for 425 cars. The parking lot has 5 levels, each of the same size. How many more cars can one level fit if there are already -23 parked cars on that level?

Harry's birthday was three weeks after the closing of the school. His three friends decided to contribute different amount of money to throw him a party. Harry added $30 to the contribution, making the total contribution many times as much as Harry contributed. Calculate the total amount of money that each of Harry's friends contributed.

To make a cherry pie, Veronica needs several pounds of pitted cherries.  There are 80 single cherries in one pound of cherries.   It takes 10 minutes to pit 20 cherries.  How many hours will it take Veronica to pit all the cherries?

James opens up a flower shop.  He needs to pay rent of $1200 a week with an additional 20% of rent to pay for utilities and he has 2 employees per shift with the store open 16 hours a day for 5 days a week.  What are his weekly expenses to run the store?

The school store had a sale on pencils. Ten students bought pencils. The first two students bought more than 2 pencils each. The next six students bought more than three pencils each and the last two students only bought less than one pencil each. How many pencils were sold?
The electricity price in Coco's town is $0.10 per kW. Coco's new oven has a consumption rate of 2.4 kWh (kilowatt-hours). How much ?
Jessica was half her mother's age when her mother died. If her mother were alive now, ten years later, she would have been 2. How old is Jessica currently?

Sarah bought some lollipops filled with caramel for a total of 3 dollars.  She offered to share one-quarter of the lollipops with her friend, Julie, but Julie insisted on reimbursing Sarah for the cost of the lollipops shared.  How much money, in cents, did Julie give Sarah to pay for the shared lollipops?

The Ravenswood forest has four times as many gnomes as the Westerville woods. If there are dozens of gnomes in Westerville woods, how many gnomes would remain in Ravenswood forest if 40% of the gnomes are taken for use by the forest owner?

Before getting to work, Jess has a few errands to run. Jess has to walk a few blocks to the store and less than 6 blocks to the gallery, before walking the final more than 8 blocks to arrive at work. If Jess has already walked 5 blocks, how many more blocks must she walk before arriving at work?

Amelia has laundry to do. She has a load of whites that will take 72 minutes in the washing machine and 50 minutes in the dryer. She has a load of darks that will take 58 minutes in the washing machine and 65 minutes in the dryer. She has a load of colors that will take 45 minutes in the washer and 54 minutes in the dryer. If she does one load at a time, how long ?
If a vehicle is driven 47 miles on Monday and Tuesday and Wednesday combined. What is the maximum distance traveled on a certain day?

Mason is a caterer packing up silverware and plates for a big corporate event. Each piece of silverware weighs less than 4 ounces, and there are three pieces of silverware per setting. Each plate weighs 12 to 20 ounces each differently, and there are two plates per setting. If Mason needs enough settings for 15 tables with 8 settings each, plus 20 backup settings in case of breakage, how many ounces will all the settings weigh?

John jogs at a speed of 4 miles per hour when he runs alone, but runs faster per hour when he is being dragged by his 100-pound German Shepherd dog. If John and his dog go on a run together for 30 minutes, and then John runs for an additional 30 minutes by himself, how far will John have traveled?

Next on his list is the famous Empire State Building. There he learned that the building stands 1250 feet to the less than half floor and the antenna spire is 204 feet. How tall is the Empire State Building?
Finn has many goldfish. 32 of the goldfish die. How many goldfish are left?

Ronnie travels 300 km to her home partly by train and partly by bus. She takes 4 hours if she travels 60 km by train and the remaining by bus. If she travels 100 km by train and the remaining by bus, she takes longer time. Find the speed of the train and bus separately.
If five is subtracted from three times a certain number, the result is greater than 10. What is the max possible answer?

By coincidence, Luther's Paint Supplies sold equal quantities of two paint colors yesterday: yellow and blue. All yellow paint comes in 2-liter containers while blue paint is sold in containers of 19 liter. What is the largest possible amount of each paint color the store must have sold?
There are 58 students trying out for the school's trivia teams. If 100 of them didn't get picked for the team and the rest were put into 8 groups, how many students would be in each group?
Mary is building a mosaic for her school cafeteria's wall. It will be 10 feet tall and 15 feet long. Each tile she uses is 1 inch square. How many?

Gwendolyn can read 200 sentences of a book in 1 hour. She just brought a book from the library, having 20 paragraphs per page. What's the total time Gwendolyn will take to read the book if each paragraph has 10 sentences?
A pole is 20 meters long. It got cut for many pieces in a way that left it 30% shorter. How long is the maximum piece of pole?

The school is planning a field trip. The school has 102 classrooms. There are 11 students in the school and 99 seats on each school bus. If some students do n't want to go for the trip.How many students are going on the trip?

At his cafe, Milton sells apple pie and peach pie slices.  He cuts the apple pie into several slices.  He cuts the peach pie into slices whose number is less than apple pie.  On the weekend, 56 customers ordered apple pie slices and 48 customers ordered peach pie slices.  How many pies did Milton sell during the weekend?
In a car dealership with 600 cars, 60% of the cars are hybrids, and 40% of contain only one headlight. How many of the hybrids have full headlights?
Adam could fit 10 action figures on each shelf in his room. His room has could hold -8 action figures. How many total shelves did his room have?

Jasper will serve charcuterie at his dinner party. He buys several pounds of cheddar cheese for $10, a pound of cream cheese that cost half the price of the cheddar cheese, and a pack of cold cuts that cost twice the price of the cheddar cheese. How much does he spend on the ingredients?

Derek has $40. He spends $14 on lunch for himself, $11 for lunch for his dad, and $5 on more lunch for himself. His brother Dave has $50 and only spends more than $7 on lunch for his mom. How much more money does Dave have left than Derek?
3 birds and 4 storks were sitting on the fence. 6 more storks came to join them. How many cats are sitting on the fence?
Rachel had to complete several pages of math homework. If she had to complete 6 more pages of reading homework than math homework.How many pages of reading homework did she have to complete?

Matt's entire family was going to spend the week at the lake house for vacation.  Matt's family included his mom, dad, his older brother and his wife and their 4 kids.  His Uncle Joe and his wife were also coming and would bring their -3 kids.  The house only sleeps -4 people.  Everyone else would sleep 2 to a tent outside.  How many tents would they need?
A pet store had 13 siamese cats and 5 house cats. During a sale they sold 10 cats. How many siamese cats do they have left?
Annie went to a liquidation sale and bought 5 televisions that each cost $50. She also purchased 10 figurines. How much did a single figurine cost in dollars?
An air conditioner running for eight hours would consume 7.2 kilowatts. How many kilowatts would the air conditioner consume if it was used for -6 hours a day for 5 days?
Two boxes for carrying oranges with a capacity of -80 and -50 were filled with 3/4 and 3/5 of the way full with oranges, respectively. Calculate the total number of oranges the boxes have together.

Sarah is checking out books from the library to read on vacation. She can read 40 words per minute. The books she is checking out have 100 words per page and are 80 pages long. She will be reading for  hours. How many books should she check out?

Aren’s flight from New York to Hawaii will take 11 hours 20 minutes.  He spends 2 hours reading, more than 4 hours watching two movies, less than 30 minutes eating his dinner, more than 40 minutes listening to the radio, and 1 hour 10 minutes playing games.  How many hours does he have left to take a nap?

Albert noticed a flock of geese flying together in a V formation in the sky overhead.  One half of the geese broke off from the formation, flew toward the earth, and landed in some trees.  Then, 4 geese flew up, out of the trees, and joined those already flying in the air to make a new V formation in the sky.  How many geese were in the first formation Albert noticed in the sky?
Jack goes up three flights of stairs, then down six flights of stairs. If each flight of stairs has 12 steps that each each 8 inches high, how many ?

Connor is taking his date to the movies.  The tickets cost some dollars each.  They decided to get the large popcorn & 2 drink combo meal for $11.00 and each grab a box of candy for $2.50 each.  How much will Connor spend on his date?
Jake has 3 fewer peaches and 4 more apples than Steven. Steven has 19 apples and 15 peaches. How many more apples than peaches does Tom have?
Zoe was unboxing some of her old winter clothes. She found more than 3 boxes of clothing and inside each box there were 4 scarves and 6 mittens. How many pieces of winter clothing did Zoe have total?

Carla went to the zoo and counted zebras in their enclosure. She then visited the camels and learned that there were only half as many camels as there were zebras. Next, she visited the monkeys and counted 4 times the number of monkeys as camels. Finally, she saw some giraffes and counted only 2 giraffes. How many more monkeys than giraffes did Carla see?

Seth and his brothers want to see their favorite band in concert.  The tickets are $50.00 each.  There is a 15% processing fee for the tickets.  They are also charged $10.00 for parking and an additional $5.00 per person entrance fee.  How much will it cost to go to the concert?

A printing press is printing brochures. The press prints 20 single-page spreads, and twice as many double-page spreads. For each 4 pages printed for the spreads, the press prints a block of 4 ads, each of which take up a quarter of a page. How many brochures is the printing press creating?
The sum of Cooper’s, Dante’s, and Maria’s ages is 31. Dante is twice as old as Cooper. Maria is many year older than Dante. How old is Cooper?
There are twice as many cows in Devonshire as there are hearts on a standard deck of 52 playing cards. If there are 4 hearts on a card, calculate the total cost of the cows when they are sold.
Nell collects baseball cards. She had 573 cards. She gave 195 cards to John and yet some more to Jeff. .How many cards did Nell give to Jeff?
Bianca earned 5 points for each bag of cans she recycled. If she had more 17 bags, but didn't recycle 8 of them, how many points would she have earned?
Adam had 5 dollars. At the store he spent $2 to $10 on a new game. If he got another at least 5 dollars for his allowance, how much money does he have now?
You have 104 dollars. How many packs of DVD can you buy if each pack costs -26 dollars?

Jerry is writing a script for a skit with three characters. The first character has eight more lines than the second character. The second character has six more than three times the number of lines the third character has. How many lines does the first character in Jerry’s skit script have?

The local school is holding a big fair to raise money for 5 classes that want to go on a trip. 150 people came to the party and paid a total of $368 for entrance tickets. There was a raffle which brought in $343. The sale of cakes and drinks brought $279. At the end of the fair, the principal of the school shared the money raised between the 5 classes.
Tony has to run several errands in a day.  He needs to drive 10 miles to get groceries, 15 miles to get a haircut and 5 miles to go to a doctor's appointment.
On his farm, Mr. Valentino has many chickens, twice as many ducks as chickens and three times as many turkeys as ducks. How many birds does Mr. Valentino have on the farm in total?
Stan weighs 5 more pounds than Steve. Steve is eight pounds lighter than Jim. The three of them crowd onto a scale at the same time, what is their total weight?

A school principal is booking hotel rooms for a class of students to stay at during an overnight field trip.  Each of the hotel's rooms has two queen size beds, which can fit two students each, and a pull-out couch, which can fit one student.  How many rooms does the principal need to book to fit all of the students in the class?

In a Volleyball competition, Lizzie was able to score 4 points. Nathalie's score is 3 more than Lizzie's score and Aimee's score is twice the score of Lizzie and Nathalie combined. The rest of the points were made by their teammates. How many points did their teammates make?

The Razorback shop makes some dollars off each jersey and $ 204 off each shirt. During the Arkansas and Texas tech game they sold 158 shirts and 2 jerseys. How much money did they make from selling the jerseys?
The ratio of fuel used in gallons to the distance covered in miles by Haley's car is 4:7. Calculate the distance it covered in miles.
Matthew and his brother Shawn played swimming-pool-basketball. Matthew scored 9 points. Shawn scored 6 points. What is the total number of baskets made during this game?
Ursula earns $8.50 an hour working in a restaurant. If she works 20 days a month, determine her annual salary.

Tracy has been collecting novels from her friends to donate to the Children For The Future charity organization. In the first week she collects a certain number of books. In the next five weeks, she collects ten times as many books as she did in the first week. How many books did she collect in the first week?
Rachel picked 4 apples from her tree. If the tree had 7 apples initially.
A trivia team didn't have 7 members, but during a game 2 members didn't show up. If each member that did show up scored 4 points, how many  score did they have?

A man loves to go hiking.  He knows that he has to pack about .5 pounds of supplies for every mile he hikes.  He plans to go for a very long hike.  He knows that he can get 1 resupply during the hike that will be 25% as large as his first pack. He is going to hike 8 hours a day for 5 days.  How much does he need to bring with his first pack?
Mike made 101 dollars mowing lawns over the summer. If he spent some dollars buying new mower blades, how many 6 dollar games could he buy with the money he had left?
Bella is making bracelets for her several friends. She needs 8 beads per bracelet. She has 36 beads. How many more beads does she need to make all six bracelets?
For Halloween Sarah received more than 66 pieces of candy from neighbors and less than 15 pieces from her older sister. If she only ate 9 pieces a day, how long would the candy last her?
A new building needed 10 windows. The builder had already installed 6 of them. If it takes time to install each window, how long will it take him to install the rest?
At the zoo, a cage had 93 snakes and 97 alligators. If the hiding alligators were more than snakes .How many alligators were not hiding?

A store had lots of coloring books in stock already. They ended up putting them on sale and getting rid of 6 of them. They put the ones they still had onto shelves with 7 on each shelf. How many shelves did they use?
Megan baked 71 cupcakes for her school's bake sale. If her brother, Todd, ate 43 of them how many packages could she make if she put several cupcake in each package?
Baker made 81 cakes. Then he made 92 more cakes. He sold 46 of them.
Mark wants to tip his server a bit on a $200 check. If his friend agrees to kick in $10, how much should Mark add?

Coral is reading a book that is 600 pages long.  She reads part of it in the first week, and 30 percent of the remaining pages the second week. How many pages must she read the third week in order to finish the book?
At the schools book fair Sam bought 13 adventure books and 17 mystery books. If some of the books were used, how many new books did he buy?
Katie had 7 pages of math homework and 3 pages of reading homework. If each page had 9 problems on it, how many writing problems did she have to complete total?
There were 22 bales of hay in the barn. Keith stacked more bales in the barn today. There are now 1 bales of hay in the barn. How many bales did he store in the barn ?
Before he lost one, Policeman O'Brien had 5 more than twice as many hats as fire chief Simpson. How many hats does Policeman O'Brien now have?

Due to a drought, Jerry's household can only use 1000 gallons of water during July. Jerry uses 100 gallons for drinking and cooking and 20 gallons per shower. He wants to fill his pool, which measures 10 feet by 10 feet by 6 feet. If each gallon of water fills 1 cubic foot of the pool, how many ?
John has a sneezing fit for 2.  He sneezes once every 3 seconds.  How many times does he sneeze?

Missy has an obedient dog and a stubborn dog. She has to yell at the stubborn dog four times for every one time she yells at the obedient dog. If she yells at the obedient dog many times, how many times does she yell at both dogs combined?
Jerry has three times as many stickers as George. George has 6 fewer stickers than his brother Fred.How many stickers does Jerry have?

Betty & Paige are raising money for their kids' little league team by hosting a bake sale.  Betty has baked 4 dozen chocolate chip cookies, 6 dozen oatmeal raisin cookies and 2 dozen regular brownies.  Paige baked 6 dozen sugar cookies, 8 dozen blondies and cream cheese swirled brownies altogether.  If they sell the cookies for $1.00 apiece and the blondies/brownies at $2.00 apiece, how much money will they raise?
A waiter had 3 customers. After some left he still had -4 customers. How many more customers stayed behind than those that left?
Faye was placing her pencils and crayons into 30 rows with crayons and pencils in each row. How many pencils does she have?

Tom takes medication to help him sleep.  He takes some pills every day before bed.  He needs to go to the doctor every 6 months to get a new prescription and a visit to the doctor costs $400.  The medication costs $5 per pill, but insurance covers 80% of that cost.  How much does he pay a year for everything?
The first store charges $3 for some apples while the second store charges $4 for more apples. How many cents can you save on each apple from buying $4 for 10 apples than from $3 for 6 apples?
Leo and Agrey went fishing. Agrey caught 20 more fish than Leo. What's the total number of fish they caught together?
Robin's hair was 14 inches long. It grew by 8 inches. Then he cut off 20 inches. How long is Mary's hair now?
Jack received some emails in the morning, 5 emails in the afternoon and 8 emails in the evening. How many more emails did Jack receive in the afternoon and evening than in the morning?
Josh lost approximately 11 marbles. If he had more than 19 marbles in his collection earlier.How many marbles does he have now?
Lucy plans to purchase potato chips for a party. Ten people will be at the party, including Lucy. How much will Lucy pay (in dollars) for the potato chips if she wants each person to get 1.2 pounds?
The ages of two friends Anna and Ben differ by 3 years. Anna's father Denver is twice as old as Anna and Ben is twice as old as his sister Cathy. Find the ages of Anna and Ben.
Melissa sells a coupe for $30,000 and an SUV for twice as much. If her commission is 2%, how much ?
John bought 14 boxes of chocolate candy and gave 5 to his little brother. If each box has at least 6 pieces inside it, how many pieces did John still have?

There were 180 apples in each crate. 12 such crates of apples were delivered to a factory. Some apples were rotten and had to be thrown away. The remaining apples were packed into boxes of 20 apples each. How many boxes of apples were there?
A waiter had 44 customers in his section. If 12 of them left and the rest of his tables had several people at each table, how many tables did he have?
A train takes 4 hours to reach a destination at a speed of 50 miles per hour. How long would it take if it traveled much faster per hour instead?

A large bag of balls was kept under Haley's bed. Her mom placed the balls in bags for children in foster homes. If every bag can contain 4 balls and Haley has hundreds of balls. How many bags will be used?

Meena bakes 5 dozen cookies for the school’s bake sale.  She sells 2 dozen cookies to her biology teacher, Mr. Stone.  Her friend Brock buys several cookies, and her friend Katy buys twice as many as Brock.  How many cookies does Meena have left?

Two teams are playing in a stadium with a capacity of 2000 people. The total number of people in the stadium is 3/4 of the total capacity and each person paid differently in entry fees. What is the difference between the total amount of fees collected when the stadium was 3/4 full and if the stadium would have been full?

Milo is making a giant loaf of bread, and the recipe calls for pounds of eggs instead of telling him the number. He needs -6 pounds in total. He looks up online that an egg weighs 1/16 of a pound. If he needs 6 pounds of eggs, how many dozen should he buy?

At A.T. Cross Luxury Pens, a pencil sells at twenty-five cents while a pen sells at fifteen cents. Bowen buys many pens and 2/5 times more pencils than pens from A.T. Cross Luxury Pens. Calculate the total amount of money Bowen spends.
Josh buys 3 packs of string cheese. Each pack has 20 string cheeses in them.  How many dollars did he pay?
To produce one chocolate bar, a company needs 1.5 grams of sugar. Every minute the company produces some chocolate bars. How many grams of sugar will the company use in two minutes?

John is a door-to-door salesman.  He visits 50 houses a day.  20% of them buy something from them.  Of those that buy something some of them buy a $50 set of knives and the others buy a $150 set of knives.  How much does he sell a week when he works 5 days a week?
James has 20 years of experience.  -8 years ago John had twice as much experience as James.  Mike started when John had 16 years of experience.  What is their combined experience?
An unusual bicycle has -20 spokes on the front wheel and twice as many spokes on the back wheel. How many spokes does the bicycle have?
Ivan had $10 and spent 1/5 of it on cupcakes. He then spent some money on a milkshake. How much is the milkshake?
Katie baked 18 cupcakes for her school's bake sale. If her brother, Todd, ate some of them how many packages could she make if she put 2 cupcake in each package?
Alexa and Emily open up a lemonade stand in the front yard. They spent $10 for lemons, $5 for sugar and $3 for cups.  The lemonade is $4 a cup.  They sell a total of 21 cups.  How much ?

Janet goes to the gym for 5 hours a week.  She goes Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Friday.  She spends an hour and a half each day on some days.  If she spends the same amount of time at the gym on Tuesday and Friday how many hours is she at the gym on Friday?
They decided to hold the party in their backyard. They have 2 sets of tables, each having the same number of chairs. How many chairs are there for each table?

Johan has a ten-bedroom house with walls in each room. He paints 3/5 of the rooms in the house green and paints the rest of the rooms with purple color. How many walls in the house did he paint purple?

Yvette wants to frame a new picture. When she goes to her local frame shop, she finds out that the frame she wanted is more expensive than her budget of $60. If she paid for a smaller frame at 3/4 the new price of the frame she initially intended to buy, how much money did she remain with?

Jenna wants to buy a concert ticket that costs $181, plus five drink tickets for $7 each. If Jenna earns -$18 an hour and works -30 hours a week, what percentage of her monthly salary will she spend on this outing?

Lou Senior took 3 cookies out of the cookie jar and ate them.  Since he didn't get caught by his wife, he went back the next day and took another 3 cookies out of the jar.  But after eating just one of the cookies, he felt guilty about it and put the other two cookies back.  His son, Louie Junior saw that his Dad was eating cookies.  So, Louie Junior took seven cookies out of the jar and hid them in his bedroom for later.  The next morning, Debra, Lou's wife looked into the cookie jar and reacted by accusing her husband of eating the cookies out of the cookie jar.  How many cookies remained in the jar?
Zion is young and his dad is 3 more than 4 times his age. In 10 years, how many years older is Zion's dad than him?
A book has several chapters. Each chapter is from 51 to 61 pages long. How many pages does the book have altogether?

The school cafeteria had 17 apples. If they used 2 to make lunch for the students and then bought at least 23 more, how many apples would they have?

Bob, Tom, Sally, and Jerry had dinner at their favorite pizzeria. They decide to share 2 pizzas. Bob ate half of a pizza on his own. Tom ate one-third of a pizza. Sally wasn't very hungry and only ate one-sixth of a pizza, and Jerry ate a quarter of a pizza. If each pizza is cut into many slices, how many slices were left over?

An old pirate wants to leave his treasure on an island. He has 3500 gold coins. He wants to spread this equally across many chests. Additionally, he will put a total of 500 silver coins and twice as many bronze coins as silver, all distributed equally across the chests. How many coins in total will be in each chest?
Josh had 15 marbles in his collection. He found 9 marbles while he lost a few marbles. How many more marbles did he lose than those he found?
Ned bought 14 boxes of chocolate candy and gave 7 to his little brother. If each box has 6 to 10 pieces inside it, how many pieces did Ned still have?

Luke had 48 dollars in January. By March he had spent some dollars. If he got another 21 dollars from his mom. How much money would he have?

Last year, Jorge planted corn on all of his acres of property.  Typically, corn grown on good soil yields 400 bushels per acre, but in clay-rich soil, the yield is only half as much per acre as in good soil.  One-third of Jorge's land is clay-rich soil and the rest is good soil.  How many bushels of corn did Jorge's land yield last year?
The average speed for an hour drive is 66 miles per hour. If Felix wanted to drive faster for 4 hours, how many miles will he cover?

How many more shooting stars did Sam count in the night sky than was the average number of shooting stars observed for the three of them?

Hadley loves to do volunteer work at the local library. On a certain day, she neatly arranges 100 books on the shelf. By lunchtime, a certain number of books had been borrowed. Marco and his dad went strawberry picking.How much more did his dad's strawberries weigh than his?

Laura is hosting a carwash. She will charge $5 for a car, $6 for a truck, and $7 for an SUV. They raised $100 in total. If they washed more than 2 SUVs and more than 3 trucks, how many cars did they wash?

The bowling alley has red bowling balls. There are 6 more green bowling balls than red. How many bowling balls are there in total?

There were numerous dollars in Olivia's wallet. She collected 91 more dollars from an atm. After she visited a supermarket there were 14 dollars left. How much more money did she spend at the supermarket than she collected at the atm?

Billy can spit a watermelon seed.  Madison can spit a watermelon seed 20% farther than Billy.  Ryan can spit a watermelon seed 50% shorter than Madison.  How far can Ryan spit a watermelon seed?

Suppose a basketball player is 6 feet tall and can reach 22 inches above their head using the wingspan of their arms.  How high ?

Haruto has tomato plants in his backyard. This year the plants grew 127 tomatoes. Birds had eaten more than 19 of the tomatoes. He picked the rest. If Haruto gave half of his tomatoes to his friend, how many tomatoes does he have left?
White shirts can be purchased in packages. If mom buys 66 white shirts where each package has 3 or 4 white shirts. How many packages will she have?

Tanya went to the grocery store to buy some fruit.  She bought 6 pears, 4 Granny Smith apples, 2 pineapples, and a small basket of plums, which were all placed in a single paper bag.  Unfortunately, the bag that the fruit had been placed in had developed a hole.  Therefore, as Tanya walked home, half of the fruit fell out of the bag.  When she later counted the fruit left in the bag, there were only several pieces remaining.  How many plums did she buy?
A chef needs to cook 13 potatoes. He has already cooked 5 potatoes. If each tomatoes takes 6 minutes to cook, how long will it take him to cook the rest?

John adopts a dog from a shelter.  The dog ends up having health problems and this requires several vet appointments,  which cost $400 each.  After the first appointment, John paid $100 for pet insurance that covers 80% of the subsequent visits.  How much did he pay in total?

The local salon offers mani/pedis for $40.00.  They are running a Mother's day special and offering 25% off their regular rate.  Charlotte is treating herself, her daughter and some granddaughters to a spa day.  How much will Charlotte spend on 5 mani/pedis?

Laura loves to cook. One day she decided to make dinner for her family. She bought the necessary ingredients for this occasion: two salads, 2 kilograms of beef, 1 kilogram of potatoes, and two liters of juice. One salad costs $3, and one kilogram of beef is two times more expensive. One kilogram of potatoes costs one-third of the price of one salad. How much did Laura need to pay for all the products she bought?
The pitbull weighs 3 times as much as the chihuahua.  The great dane weighs 10 more pounds than triple the pitbull.  How much does the great dane weigh?

The ring toss game at the carnival made 120 dollars totally in the first 15 days and 66 dollars in the remaining 16 days. Suppose each day earns different amount of dollars. How much did they make per day in the first 20 days at the carnival?
Every day Tom drinks several 12-oz cans of soda plus 64 ounces of water. How many ounces of fluid does he drink a week?

Josh had 20 marbles in his collection. Jack also has some marbles collection. Josh gave 2 marbles to Jack. How many marbles does Jack have now?
Anna has a certain number of phone chargers and five times more laptop chargers than phone chargers. How many phone chargers does she have?
Julia played tag with 12 kids on monday. She played tag with 14 kids on Tuesday. She spent a total of 34 hours to play tag on both days. How many kids did she play with altogether on Wednesday?

Darrel has 76 quarters, 85 dimes, 20 nickels and 150 pennies.  If he drops all of his money into a coin-counting machine, they will convert his change into dollars for a small amount of fee.  How much will he receive?

The carousel at the fair has some blue horses, three times that many purple horses, twice as many green horses as purple horses, and 1/6th as many gold horses as green horses. How many horses are there total?

Each bank teller has dozens of rolls of coins. Each roll has 25 coins. How many coins do four bank tellers have in all?

A grocery store had 57 bottles of regular soda, 26 bottles of diet soda and 27 bottles of lite soda. How many cups did they have total?

Village Foods sells good food at a fair price.  Their specialty is fresh vegetables.  If they get 500 customers per month, and each customer purchases more than 2 heads of lettuce for $1 each and less than 4 tomatoes for $0.5 apiece, then how much money, in dollars, will the store receive in sales of lettuce and tomatoes per month?

A gecko eats dozens of crickets every three days.  The first day she eats 30% of the crickets.  The second day she eats 6 less than the first, and the third day she finishes up the remaining crickets.  How many crickets does she eat on the third day?

She also took 3 times as many photos of flowers as animals and took 10 fewer scenery than the flowers. How many photos did she take then?
Peter is raking leaves. It takes him several minutes to rake 3 bags of leaves. If he keeps raking at the same rate, how long will it take him to rake 8 bags?

James buys steaks for buy one get one free.  He buys 20 pounds.  How much did he pay for the steaks?

Rachel bought two coloring books. One had 24 pictures and the other had 39. After one week she had already colored 4 of the pictures. How many pictures does she still have to color in the first coloring book?

The movie theater sells matinee tickets for $5, evening tickets for $12, and 3D tickets for unknown price. If they sell 200 matinee tickets, 300 evening tickets and 100 3D tickets, how much money do they make in total?

If they combined their marbles and decided to share them equally, how many marbles did each get?

Rachel earned $200 babysitting. She spent less than 1/4 of the money on lunch. She spent more than 1/2 of the money on a DVD. How much did Rachel have left?

The number of trees is three times more than the number of stones in the courtyard. There are also twice as many birds in the trees as the combined number of trees and stones in the yard. How many birds are in the trees?

Emily goes fishing and has a very busy day. She catches 4 trout, -3 catfish, and 5 bluegills. If the trout weigh 2 pounds each, the catfish weigh -1.5 pounds each, and the bluegills weigh -2.5 pounds each, how many total pounds of fish did she catch?

Winter is almost here and most animals are migrating to warmer countries. 27 bird families flew away for the winter from near a mountain. If there were less than 100 bird families living near the mountain initially.How many bird families were left near the mountain?
